canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, ComplexWebQuestions, also referred to as CWQ, is a benchmark dataset characterized by its use of "multi-hop, web-based questions." The available sources consistently frame it as an instrument for testing reasoning abilities. One paper explicitly defines its purpose as testing a model's "reasoning ability over web content." In practice, it is used to measure several related constructs. The data shows it is used to operationalize and assess both "Complex reasoning" and "Commonsense understanding." Thus, across the surveyed work, the benchmark is positioned as a tool for evaluating different facets of reasoning over information from the web.
papers: Mixture of Weight-shared Heterogeneous Group Attention Experts for Dynamic Token-wiseKVOptimization
PRISM: Efficient Long-Range Reasoning With Short-ContextLLMs

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset, also known as CWQ, that contains multi-hop, web-based questions designed to test reasoning ability over web content.
definitions.paper: PRISM: Efficient Long-Range Reasoning With Short-ContextLLMs

relationships.paper: Mixture of Parrots: Experts improve memorization more than reasoning
